title: Clinical Randomisation of an Anti-fibrinolytic in Symptomatic Mild Head Injury in Older Adults

skipped_criteria: History or evidence of head injury (e.g. laceration, bruise, swelling or pain in head or face): no schema field captures physical exam findings on the head/face.
Has one or more of: impaired consciousness (loss of consciousness, amnesia, confusion), nausea or vomiting: no schema fields exist for amnesia, confusion, nausea, or vomiting as discrete symptoms.
Within 3 hours of injury: temporal logic relative to injury time cannot be expressed with the available fields and operators.

Do not include if injury time interval cannot be estimated (e.g. unwitnessed fall, patient found on floor): requires knowledge of witness status and ability to confirm injury time, which are not schema fields.
Not living in a nursing home, mental health institution, or prison: no schema field captures residential or custodial status.
Patient will be conveyed to or is admitted to a participating hospital: logistical/site criterion not expressible in the schema.
TXA not clearly indicated (e.g. major bleeding) or contraindicated (e.g. suspected stroke): medication indication/contraindication logic and stroke suspicion are not schema fields.
